Sub-Steps: Vertical Stud
Review wall layout marks on bottom and top plates for stud spacing
Measure required stud length between plates and mark cut line on lumber
Cut stud to length with power saw
Set stud on bottom plate layout mark and tip into place under top plate
Tap stud plumb and verify alignment with level
Fasten stud to plates with nails or screws through toe-nailing or metal connectors
King Stud
Confirm opening width on plans and transfer king stud locations to plates
Measure floor-to-plate height and cut king studs to full wall height
Stand each king stud against layout line flanking rough opening
Check plumb with level and adjust as needed
Secure king studs to bottom and top plates with nails or structural screws
Brace temporarily until adjoining members lock studs in place
Lower Cripple Stud
Measure distance from bottom plate to underside of window sill plate
Cut cripple stud pieces to measured length
Position cripples at marked on-center spacing below sill opening
Hold each cripple tight between bottom plate and sill plate
Nail or screw through plates into each cripple end
Verify top of sill remains level after fastening
OSB Sheathing Plate
Measure wall panel area and mark OSB panel cut lines to fit between framing edges and openings
Cut OSB sheets to size with circular saw, maintaining square edges
Erect scaffold or use ladders for safe access to wall height
Position first sheet flush with bottom plate and edge of framing
Hold panel tight, check for plumb and proper edge alignment
Fasten OSB to studs, plates, and blocking with specified nails or screws at required spacing, starting from center and working outward
Stagger vertical joints between courses and continue installing successive sheets
Recheck flatness and joint alignment, adding additional fasteners where needed
Top Plate
Lay out stud and opening locations on top plate stock using tape and pencil
Cut plates to wall length, including splices as needed
Set first top plate on edge along wall line and tack to stud tops
Install second (cap) plate overlapping joints for strength
Nail or screw plates to each stud end and to adjoining plates at corners and partitions
Verify straightness of plate line and correct any bows with blocking or bracing
Jack Stud
Measure required height from bottom plate to underside of header
Cut jack studs to length with square ends
Place each jack stud against king stud at opening sides
Align bottom with layout mark and ensure top meets header seat line
Nail jack studs to king studs and bottom plate, then toenail into header after placement
Confirm assembly is plumb and secure
Bear Loading
Check plan for header span and laminate requirements
Measure opening width and cut header members to length
Assemble multiple header plies with adhesive and nails or screws as specified
Lift header onto jack studs within opening using additional help or temporary support
Set header flush with top of jack studs and centered over opening
Nail or screw header to jack studs and add metal hangers or straps if required
Verify header is level and bearing fully on both jacks
L/U Stud
Locate position adjacent to first vertical stud per layout
Measure floor-to-plate height and cut utility stud to length
Stand stud in place providing space for service penetrations as detailed
Plumb stud with level and align faces flush with neighboring framing
Fasten stud to plates and add blocking or straps for lateral bracing as required
Bore service holes at designated heights for mechanical or electrical runs
Lower Sill Plate
Mark sill height on king studs according to window schedule
Cut sill plate to span between jack studs
Position sill on marked lines with crown up and faces flush
Tack sill to king or jack studs, then secure with nails or screws through end joints
Check sill for level across width of opening
Add temporary support or shims if adjustments are required
Bottom Plate
Snap chalk line on subfloor to establish wall location
Lay out stud and opening positions along bottom plate stock
Cut plates to required lengths including splice pieces
Position bottom plate on layout line and drill for anchor locations if needed
Fasten plate to floor framing or slab with nails, screws, or anchors per specification
Verify plate alignment and secure temporary braces until studs are installed
Top Sill Plate
Measure distance between king studs above window opening
Cut top sill plate to length ensuring square ends
Mark seat line on king studs at specified elevation
Set top sill on marks and hold flush with inner faces of studs
Nail or screw plate to king studs and to any top cripple studs above
Confirm plate is level and properly tied into surrounding framing
Top Cripple Stud
Determine distance from top sill or header to underside of top plate
Cut cripple studs to required length
Position cripples at on-center spacing above opening between header and top plate
Ensure each cripple is plumb and flush with wall face
Fasten cripples through top plate and header with nails or screws
Verify continuous load path by checking contact at both ends
